title: 10 best herbal creams for low blood pressure

remedy_properties: Adonixanthin: This antioxidant-rich compound helps to strengthen blood vessel walls, which can improve circulation and increase blood pressure by reducing vascular resistance.
Cyclopropenoids: These compounds have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory effects, helping to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help to alleviate low blood pressure symptoms.

Fatty acids: The fatty acid content in Maidenhair Tree creams, particularly oleic acid, has been found to have a hypotensive effect, meaning it can help to lower blood pressure by relaxing blood vessel walls and improving circulation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with low blood pressure because of its vasodilatory properties. The cream increases blood flow and circulation by dilating blood vessels, which helps to improve oxygenation of the skin. This, in turn, can help to reduce symptoms of low blood pressure such as dizziness and fainting. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant properties may help to protect blood vessels from damage, promoting overall cardiovascular health and potentially alleviating low blood pressure symptoms. Regular use may also improve overall circulation and energy levels.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds help improve blood flow and circulation, which can contribute to increased blood pressure.
Terpenoids (Bilobalide): Bilobalide has been shown to have a vasodilatory effect, which helps to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, thereby potentially alleviating low blood pressure.
Bilobin (Ginkgolides): Ginkgolides have been found to improve blood vessel function and reduce vascular resistance, which can help increase blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are commonly used to make creams for low blood pressure due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help improve circulation and blood flow.
Barks: The barks of Ginkgo biloba are used to make creams for low blood pressure due to their ability to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation.

Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are used to make creams for low blood pressure due to their potential to lower blood pressure and improve circulation by inhibiting the enzyme that constricts blood vessels.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of ginkgo biloba extract powder and 100 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Melt the beeswax in the double boiler while stirring occasionally for 10 minutes.
Add 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of vitamin e oil to the melted beeswax mixture.
Combine the ginkgo biloba extract powder with 50 grams of distilled water to form a paste.
Gradually add the paste to the melted beeswax mixture and stir continuously for 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Cardiovascular protection: Ginseng creams contain compounds like ginsenosides and panaxadiol that help to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation, which can help to increase blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease.

Adaptogenic properties: Ginseng has adaptogenic properties that allow it to help the body respond to stress and promote balance in bodily functions, including blood pressure regulation, which can help to stabilize and normalize blood pressure levels.

Vasodilatory effects: The active compounds in ginseng creams, such as panaxyloline, have been shown to dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help to increase blood pressure and reduce the risk of hypotension (low blood pressure).
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng, also known as ginseng, creams helps with low blood pressure because of its stimulating properties that increase blood flow and circulation. The active compounds in ginseng, such as ginsenosides, have been shown to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health by dilating blood vessels and improving microcirculation. This allows more oxygen and nutrients to reach the body's tissues and organs, ultimately helping to regulate blood pressure and improve overall energy levels. Regular use of ginseng creams may also promote a sense of well-being and vitality.
remedy_constituents: Ginsenosides: These triterpenoid saponins help improve blood pressure by increasing the production of nitric oxide, which relaxes blood vessels and improves blood flow, thereby increasing blood pressure.

Panaxynol: This phenolic compound has been shown to have a stimulating effect on the cardiovascular system, which helps to increase blood pressure by improving the heart's pumping ability and enhancing blood vessel constriction.

Ginsenolic acid: This phenolic acid has been found to have a vasoconstrictive effect, meaning it helps to constrict blood vessels, which can help increase blood pressure in individuals with low blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are rich in ginsenosides, which have been shown to help increase blood pressure by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation.
Rhyzomes: They also contain ginsenosides and have been traditionally used to enhance cardiovascular health and improve blood pressure.
Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to improve blood vessel function and increase blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Combine 10 grams of dried panax ginseng root with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a clean glass container.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 30 minutes at 60-70 degrees celsius to facilitate extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another container to remove solids.
Add 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of emulsifying wax to the mixture and heat it at 70-80 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound helps to increase blood volume and blood pressure by inhibiting the activity of an enzyme called 11β-hydroxysteroid dehydrogenase, which converts cortisol into a form that can lower blood pressure.
Flavonoids: These antioxidants help to improve cardiovascular health by reducing inflammation and improving blood vessel function, both of which can contribute to low blood pressure.

Saponins: Saponins, particularly the triterpenoid glycosides present in licorice root, have been shown to improve blood flow and increase blood pressure by dilating blood vessels and improving vascular tone.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with low blood pressure because of its unique ability to help regulate and stabilize blood pressure levels. The active compound glycyrrhizin in the cream has been shown to have a vasoconstrictive effect, which helps to constrict blood vessels and improve blood flow to the extremities. Additionally, glycyrrhizin has been found to have an adaptogenic effect, which helps the body to adapt to stress and maintain homeostasis, ultimately leading to improved blood pressure regulation. This makes it a valuable natural remedy for individuals experiencing low blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: It helps with low blood pressure by increasing aldosterone levels, which in turn increases sodium reabsorption in the kidneys, leading to increased blood volume and blood pressure.

Flavonoids (specifically, isoorientin and orientin): They help with low blood pressure by exerting vasodilatory effects, causing blood vessels to relax and widen, which increases blood flow and subsequently helps to stabilize blood pressure.

Saponins (specifically, glycyrrhizic acid): They help with low blood pressure by enhancing the activity of the renin-angiotensin-aldosterone system, which regulates blood pressure and electrolyte balance, ultimately contributing to increased blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are a primary source of glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps to increase blood pressure due to its mineralocorticoid properties.
Leaves: They are sometimes used to provide additional benefits, although they are less commonly used than roots.
Seeds: They are sometimes used to enhance the effects of glycyrrhizin, although they are not as widely used as roots.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly to remove impurities.
Grind the roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the powder with 100 grams of sweet almond oil and 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally to achieve a smooth consistency.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth into a clean container and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks.

remedy_properties: Cardiac tonic: Dong Quai's cardiac tonic effects help to increase blood circulation, which can improve blood flow and raise blood pressure in individuals with hypotension (low blood pressure). This property helps to strengthen the heart and improve its ability to pump blood efficiently.

Vasodilator: The vasodilatory properties of Dong Quai creams cause blood vessels to dilate or widen, which increases blood flow and can help to raise blood pressure in individuals with hypotension. By reducing blood vessel constriction, Dong Quai cream may also reduce the risk of cardiovascular complications.

Adaptogenic: Dong Quai's adaptogenic properties help the body to adapt to stress and other factors that can contribute to low blood pressure. This property enables the body to respond more effectively to changes in blood pressure and maintain a healthy balance, rather than experiencing extreme fluctuations.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica sinensis, also known as dong quai, creams helps with low blood pressure because of its unique blend of active compounds. The cream is rich in ferulic acid and other flavonoids that help to improve blood circulation and stimulate the body's natural response to low blood pressure. The herbs in the cream also have a vasodilatory effect, causing blood vessels to widen and improve blood flow to the extremities. This natural combination helps to increase blood pressure and alleviate symptoms associated with low blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: This sesquiterpene lactone helps with low blood pressure by dilating blood vessels and improving circulation, thereby increasing blood flow and oxygen delivery to vital organs.

Butylphthalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has a vasodilatory effect, which helps to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, thereby increasing blood pressure and preventing dizziness or lightheadedness.

N-butylidenephthalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has a stimulating effect on the cardiovascular system, which helps to increase heart rate and blood pressure, thereby counteracting the effects of low blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used for their warming and nourishing properties, which help to tonify the blood and promote circulation.
Stems: They are used to regulate the blood vessels and improve blood flow, which helps to alleviate low blood pressure.
Leaves: They are used for their antioxidant properties and ability to promote blood circulation, which helps to prevent blood pressure from dropping.
remedy_recipe: Gather 50g of dried angelica sinensis roots and 250ml of jojoba oil for the recipe.
Grind the dried angelica sinensis roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground powder with 100g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter in a heat-proof bowl.
Melt the beeswax and shea butter mixture in a double boiler at 160°f for 10 minutes while stirring occasionally.
Mix the melted mixture with the jojoba oil and allow it to cool and thicken for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effect: Herbal rosemary creams contain compounds like carnosic acid, which can help dilate blood vessels and improve blood flow to the brain and other vital organs, thereby increasing blood pressure.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Rosemary's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosmarinic acid, may help reduce inflammation in blood vessel walls, which can contribute to high blood pressure, and promote healthy circulation.

Antioxidant activity: Rosemary's antioxidants, including camphor and bornyl acetate, help neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and contribute to hypertension, supporting overall cardiovascular health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary, creams helps with low blood pressure because of its vasodilatory properties. The active compounds in rosemary, such as carnosic acid, help to relax and widen blood vessels, improving circulation and increasing blood flow to vital organs. This natural response helps to counteract the symptoms of low blood pressure, including dizziness and fatigue. By promoting healthy blood flow and circulation, rosemary creams can provide relief and support for individuals experiencing low blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic acid: This phenolic compound helps with low blood pressure by increasing blood flow and relaxing blood vessels, thereby improving cardiovascular function.
Carnosic acid: This phenolic diterpene has antioxidant and vasodilatory properties, which help to reduce blood pressure by increasing blood flow and relaxing blood vessels.

Rosmarinus officinalis extract's Terpene content (mainly borneol and borneol acetate): Terpenes, particularly borneol and borneol acetate, have been found to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health by dilating blood vessels and reducing blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in essential oils, particularly rosmarinic acid, which helps to improve circulation and increase blood pressure.
Flowers: Contain flavonoids and volatile oils that can help to dilate blood vessels and improve cardiovascular health.
Buds: Similar to leaves and flowers, buds contain rosmarinic acid and other compounds that can help to improve circulation and increase blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30g of fresh rosmarinus officinalis leaves at dawn when the dew is still present on the plant.
Steep the harvested leaves in 100ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil at 60-70 degrees celsius for 2-3 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Mix 10g of beeswax with 20g of shea butter in a double boiler at 50-60 degrees celsius until fully dissolved.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax mixture and whip until thickened to a desired consistency.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Chaste Tree cream may help protect the skin from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to various skin issues.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Vitex agnus castus, also known as chaste tree, creams helps with low blood pressure because of its adaptogenic properties and ability to regulate hormonal balance. The herb is believed to stimulate the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal (HPA) axis, which plays a crucial role in regulating blood pressure. By promoting a healthy balance of hormones, such as cortisol and insulin, the cream may help to stabilize blood pressure and alleviate symptoms associated with low blood pressure, including dizziness and fatigue. remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds may help alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure by regulating blood flow and improving cardiovascular function, although more research is needed to confirm their effects.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Vitex agnus castus, including isovitexin and isoorientin, have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate the underlying causes of low blood pressure and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to help regulate blood pressure.
Seeds: Utilized for their iridoid glycosides, specifically agnuside, which may contribute to the plant's vasodilatory effects.
Flowers: Employed for their flavonoids and saponins, which may aid in blood vessel relaxation and pressure regulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of dried vitex agnus castus flowers in late summer when they are fully ripe.
Combine the dried flowers with 30 grams of beeswax and 90 grams of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the bowl from the heat and let it cool for 30 minutes before straining the mixture through a cheesecloth.
Store the final cream in a clean glass jar in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedy_properties: Vasodilatory effects: Herbal ginger creams contain compounds like gingerols and shogaols, which have been shown to relax blood vessels and increase blood flow, helping to alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure such as dizziness and fatigue.
Cardiovascular stimulants: Ginger has been found to stimulate the heart and improve circulation, increasing blood volume and pressure, which can help counteract the effects of low blood pressure.

The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerol and shogaol, help to increase blood flow and circulation, which can aid in raising blood pressure levels. Additionally, ginger has a natural ability to constrict blood vessels, further contributing to its potential benefits for individuals with low blood pressure. remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpenoid compounds found in ginger help to improve blood circulation and increase heart rate, thereby aiding in the treatment of low blood pressure.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols are also sesquiterpenoid compounds that help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, which can help to alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure.

remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of gingerol, which helps to improve circulation and reduce blood pressure.

remedy_recipe: Infuse 100g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes in 500ml of distilled water for 2 hours.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Combine 250g of coconut oil with 100g of beeswax in a heat-proof bowl over low heat.
Add 50ml of the infused zingiber officinale liquid to the coconut oil and beeswax mixture.
Allow the mixture to solidify and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Hypericum perforatum (St John's Wort) extract: Contains hyperforin, a compound that helps improve cardiovascular circulation and increases blood pressure by dilating blood vessels.
Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in St John's Wort creams can help relax blood vessel walls, leading to increased blood flow and improved blood pressure regulation.

Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort's antioxidant compounds help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to low blood pressure by reducing blood vessel constriction.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St. John's Wort, creams helps with low blood pressure because of its vasoconstrictive properties. The active compounds in the plant, such as hyperforin and hypericin, work to constrict blood vessels, thereby increasing blood pressure. This effect can help counteract the symptoms of low blood pressure, including dizziness and fainting. remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A potent phenolic compound that has vasoprotective properties, helping to improve blood vessel function and increase blood pressure.

Oligo- and polyphenols: These compounds, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have antioxidant properties that can help protect the endothelium and improve vascular function, contributing to increased blood pressure.

remedy_parts: Leaves: High in hypericin and hyperforin, compounds that help increase blood pressure by constricting blood vessels.

remedy_recipe: Dry 250 grams of hypericum perforatum flowers in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Combine 50 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers with 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture over a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is melted.
Add 10 grams of vitamin e oil and 10 grams of aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into glass jars and let it cool and solidify for at least 30 minutes before use.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties can help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often associated with low blood pressure, and may also help to improve blood flow and circulation.
Anxiolytic: Lavender's calming and anxiolytic effects can help to reduce stress and anxiety, both of which are common triggers for low blood pressure, and promote relaxation and overall well-being.
Vasodilatory: Lavender's ability to dilate blood vessels (vasodilation) can help to increase blood flow and circulation, improving blood pressure and overall cardiovascular health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with low blood pressure because they contain compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have vasodilatory properties. These compounds help to relax and dilate blood vessels, improving circulation and increasing blood flow to the heart. By enhancing the body's natural response to low blood pressure, English lavender creams can promote a sense of calm and stability, reducing the risk of dizziness and other symptoms associated with low blood pressure. remedy_constituents: Linalool: Acts as a vasodilator, helping to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, which can help alleviate low blood pressure symptoms.
Linalyl acetate: Exhibits a sedative effect, reducing stress and anxiety, which can be a contributing factor to low blood pressure, and promoting overall cardiovascular health.

remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in essential oils, which help improve circulation and increase blood pressure.

remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers and 200ml of distilled water to make herbal tea infusion.
Steep the dried flowers in boiling water for 10 minutes to release their essential oils and properties slowly.
Strain the herbal tea mixture and discard the solids to separate the liquid from the plant material completely.
Mix 20g of beeswax and 20g of candelilla wax with 10ml of coconut oil in a double boiler slowly.
Combine 20ml of the herbal tea infusion with the wax mixture and heat it gently until it thickens into a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Vasodilatory compounds: Herbal teas like hawthorn, passionflower, and valerian root contain vasodilatory compounds that help relax blood vessels, improving blood flow and increasing blood pressure.

Adaptogenic agents: Herbs like ginseng, ashwagandha, and rhodiola rosea have adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress, reducing its negative impact on blood pressure and promoting overall cardiovascular health.

Cardiac tonics: Herbal teas like dandelion root, hawthorn, and motherwort have been traditionally used as cardiac tonics, which helps strengthen the heart and improve its function, ultimately increasing blood pressure.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with low blood pressure because it contains high levels of flavonoids, which have been shown to have a positive effect on cardiovascular health. The flavonoids in Camellia sinensis creams help to improve blood flow and increase the production of nitric oxide, a key molecule that helps to regulate blood pressure. By promoting healthy blood vessel function, Camellia sinensis creams can help to stabilize blood pressure and provide relief from symptoms of low blood pressure.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These flavonoid compounds help increase blood pressure by improving cardiovascular health, reducing inflammation, and enhancing blood vessel function.

Theaflavins: These polyphenolic compounds, formed during the fermentation process of green tea, help boost blood pressure by improving cardiovascular health, reducing inflammation, and enhancing blood vessel function.

Gallocatechin: This catechin derivative has been shown to have vasopressor effects, meaning it helps constrict blood vessels and increase blood pressure, which can be beneficial in cases of low blood pressure.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Camellia sinensis are used due to their high content of flavonoids, which have been shown to improve blood circulation and help regulate blood pressure.
Flowers: The flowers of Camellia sinensis are used for their antioxidant properties, which help to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation, thus alleviating low blood pressure symptoms.

Barks: The barks of Camellia sinensis are used due to their high content of catechins, which have been shown to have a positive effect on blood pressure by improving circulation and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Steep 1 tablespoon of camellia sinensis leaves in 8 ounces of boiling water for 5-7 minutes to create a tea.
Strain the tea through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl to remove solids.
Combine 1/2 cup of distilled water with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of candelilla wax in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of the camellia sinensis tea to the wax mixture and stir until melted and well combined.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and set at room temperature for 30 minutes to an hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder creams helps with low blood pressure because of its unique blend of herbal ingredients that work synergistically to improve circulation and increase blood flow. The elderflower, in particular, is a natural vasodilator that helps to widen blood vessels, allowing more oxygenated blood to reach the extremities. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to soften and strengthen capillaries, further enhancing blood flow and promoting overall cardiovascular health. remedy_properties: Vasodilatory compounds: Elderberry contains vasodilatory compounds like flavonoids, which help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, increasing blood flow to vital organs and tissues.

Antioxidant activity: Elderberry's high antioxidant content helps to protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to low blood pressure by damaging blood vessel walls and disrupting blood flow.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to improve circulation and regulate blood pressure.
Barks: Contain compounds like iridoid glycosides and sesquiterpenes that help to strengthen blood vessel walls and improve circulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest sambucus nigra flowers and leaves according to their optimal drying conditions for 14-21 days.
Combine dried sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in a 2:1 ratio and grind into fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix ground sambuscus nigra mixture with coconut oil and beeswax in a 70:15:15 ratio by weight and heat at 160-180°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove any sediment and discard solids within 5-7 days.
Pour the filtered cream into sterilized glass jars and store in a cool dark place for up to 6 months or until opened.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese foxglove creams helps with low blood pressure because they contain herbs such as Scrophularia and Leonurus, which have been traditionally used in Chinese medicine to tonify (strengthen) the Blood and Qi. By doing so, these herbs help to increase blood circulation, reduce fatigue, and promote overall vitality. The cream's topical application allows for direct delivery of the herbal extract to the skin, where it can be absorbed and utilized by the body to address underlying deficiencies that may be contributing to low blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Cardiac glycosides: These compounds have been shown to improve cardiac function by increasing contractility and reducing heart rate, which can be beneficial for individuals with heart conditions such as atrial fibrillation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Foxglove extracts have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which could help reduce inflammation in blood vessels and improve circulation, potentially benefiting individuals with low blood pressure.
Antioxidant activity: The plant's antioxidant compounds may help protect against oxidative stress and damage to blood vessels, which can contribute to the development of cardiovascular diseases.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Chinese Foxglove plant contain bioactive compounds that help to stimulate blood circulation and improve overall cardiovascular health, making them a key ingredient in medicinal creams for low blood pressure.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can help to alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure such as dizziness and fatigue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried rehmannia glutinosa roots weighing approximately 250 grams.
Combine the dried roots with 1000 milliliters of distilled water and soak for eight hours.
Simmer the mixture in a pot over low heat for two hours and strain the liquid.
Mix one part of the filtered liquid with four parts of aloe vera gel and refrigerate overnight.
Whip the refrigerated mixture into a smooth consistency using an electric mixer and transfer to glass jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with low blood pressure because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate circulation, increase blood flow, and enhance overall cardiovascular health. The natural diuretic properties of dandelion root and leaves help to balance fluid levels in the body, reducing blood vessel constriction and increasing blood volume, which can help to raise blood pressure to a healthy range. This holistic approach can be particularly beneficial for individuals who experience frequent episodes of low blood pressure due to medication or underlying medical conditions.
remedy_properties: Cardiotonic properties: Dandelion cream's cardiotonic compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, help to strengthen and support the heart's function, which can help improve blood circulation and increase blood pressure.

Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids and saponins present in dandelion cream have vasodilatory properties, causing blood vessels to relax and dilate, which helps to increase blood flow and raise blood pressure.

Adaptogenic properties: Dandelion's adaptogenic compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and triterpenoids, help the body adapt to stress and promote balance in physiological processes, including blood pressure regulation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in potassium, calcium, and magnesium, which can help regulate blood pressure and improve overall cardiovascular health.
Buds: Contain antioxidants and flavonoids that may help relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots and leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or sun drying method.
Grind 20 grams of dried dandelion root and 10 grams of dried dandelion leaf into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 5 grams of the powdered herb blend with 50 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler preparation.
Allow the mixture to infuse for 2 hours at room temperature or overnight in the refrigerator before straining and packaging.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe creams helps with low blood pressure because of its unique combination of vitamins, minerals, and amino acids. The gel extracted from the aloe plant has been found to help regulate blood flow and circulation, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals with low blood pressure. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also aid in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, further supporting overall cardiovascular health and blood vessel function. remedy_properties: Vasodilation: Herbal aloe creams containing ingredients like cayenne pepper or ginger have vasodilating properties, which help to dilate blood vessels and increase blood flow to the heart, thereby improving blood circulation and raising blood pressure.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in herbal aloe creams, such as vitamin C and E, help to neutralize free radicals that can damage blood vessels and contribute to low blood pressure. By reducing oxidative stress, these antioxidants can help improve blood vessel function and overall cardiovascular health.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal aloe creams containing anti-inflammatory herbs like turmeric or willow bark can help reduce inflammation in the blood vessels, which can contribute to low blood pressure. remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, aloe leaves help regulate blood flow and improve overall circulation.
Bark: Contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that help strengthen blood vessels and reduce blood pressure.
remedy_recipe: Gather organic aloe vera gel, coconut oil, ghee, and essential oils of lavender and peppermint in specified proportions.
Melt one cup of ghee in a double boiler or a heat-resistant glass bowl set over simmering water for 30 minutes.
Add two tablespoons of grated aloe vera gel to the melted ghee, stirring well to combine and allow it to infuse for 15 minutes.
In a separate bowl, mix one cup of coconut oil with five drops of lavender essential oil and three drops of peppermint essential oil until well combined.
Gradually pour the infused aloe vera mixture into the coconut oil blend while whipping it with an electric mixer or whisk for 10 minutes to emulsify the cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with low blood pressure because of its natural ability to constrict blood vessels, which in turn increases blood pressure. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties also help to relax blood vessels and improve circulation, further supporting healthy blood pressure levels. Additionally, pot marigold contains flavonoids, which have been shown to strengthen blood vessel walls and improve overall cardiovascular health, making it a natural and effective remedy for managing low blood pressure.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Pot marigold cream's antioxidant properties help to combat oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to low blood pressure by reducing blood vessel constriction and improving overall cardiovascular health.

Vasodilatory effects: The flavonoids present in pot marigold have been shown to exhibit vasodilatory properties, which can help to widen blood vessels and increase blood flow, thereby alleviating symptoms of low blood pressure such as dizziness and lightheadedness.

Cardio-protective properties: Pot marigold's cardio-protective properties may help to reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease, which is often associated with low blood pressure, by protecting against lipid peroxidation, inflammation, and oxidative stress in the heart and blood vessels.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the pot marigold plant are used due to their ability to increase peripheral resistance and improve circulation, helping to raise blood pressure.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes, or underground stems, are used because they contain bioactive compounds that help to strengthen the cardiovascular system and improve blood flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest calendula flowers, collecting enough for 100g of dried petals.
Combine 20g of dried calendula petals with 200ml of olive oil and heat for 4 hours.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container.
Add 50ml of beeswax to the calendula-infused oil, melting in a double boiler over low heat.
Whisk the mixture until smooth, pouring into jars and storing in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for low blood pressure are topical remedies infused with herbs and natural ingredients that help alleviate symptoms of hypotension. These creams typically contain herbs like ginseng, ashwagandha, and yarrow, which work to increase blood pressure and circulation. Examples include creams containing Siberian ginseng and cayenne pepper, which have been shown to improve blood pressure and overall well-being, reducing dizziness and fatigue, and improving daily activities and quality of life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to low blood pressure that are treated with herbal creams are conditions characterized by circulatory issues, such as Raynaud's disease and poor circulation. Herbal creams containing ingredients like Ginkgo biloba, Ginger, and Cayenne pepper can help improve blood flow and circulation. Other ailments, including varicose veins, bruising, and swelling, may also benefit from the use of herbal creams rich in anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, such as Arnica and Calendula.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with low blood pressure is a blend of Ginkgo Biloba, Hawthorn, and Ginger. Ginkgo Biloba improves blood flow, while Hawthorn enhances heart function and increases blood pressure. Ginger, rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, helps to reduce inflammation and promote circulation. When applied topically as creams or ointments, these herbs can work together to stimulate circulation, increase blood pressure, and promote overall cardiovascular health. Regular use may help alleviate symptoms of low blood pressure.
